Hmm, so the key points here are the location of the bulge and the relation to the menstrual cycle.

But wait, hematomas can occur from trauma, which isn't mentioned here.
The fact that the swelling increases and pain worsens with menses makes me think it's related to the menstrual cycle.
I remember that during menstruation, the uterus contracts, which can lead to retrograde flow of blood into the surrounding tissues.
This can cause a collection of blood in the posterior vaginal wall, which is called a hematocolpos.
The bluish color is due to the pooled blood, and the pain and swelling would get worse just before the period starts because of the increased blood flow or pressure from the menstrual cycle.
So, putting it all together, the most likely diagnosis is a hematocolpos.
It's a condition where blood accumulates in the vagina behind the cervix, often due to menstrual-related issues.
The symptoms align with this condition, so I'm pretty confident that's the right diagnosis.
